title: Quantum-Inspired Feature Maps Dequantize in Vision Transformer Attention: A Matched-Budget Study — code and data
description: Code, experiment scripts, and logged results for the matched-budget dequantization study of quantum-inspired feature maps (an angle/product map with encoding-depth knob L and an entangling IQP map with coupling knob c) deployed as linear-attention operators in a standard Vision Transformer. At matched parameters, FLOPs, and tuning budget, neither quantum lever beats tuned classical random features on CIFAR-100 or PathMNIST; the apparent depth advantage is a bandwidth-tuning artifact (Bochner's theorem), and entanglement yields no separation while measurably concentrating the kernel. No claim of physical quantum advantage is made: the method is classical and quantum kernels are its derivation.

creators.name: Hota, Rishabh
creators.affiliation: Simplilearn Americas, Inc., Plano, TX, USA
